summary: Bangladesh is observing an eleven-day Eid holiday period with Bangabandhu's birth anniversary marked today, while transport restrictions take effect to manage holiday traffic. Domestically, the government faces ongoing governance challenges including concerns over partisan academic appointments at universities and administrator placements in local government replacing elections, alongside falling student enrolment rates and allegations of religious freedom concerns raised in international reports. Economically, Bangladesh confronts inflationary pressures as global oil prices surge past $100 amid Middle East tensions, straining domestic fuel supplies despite increased provision, while the World Bank pledges support for job creation and inclusive growth. The central bank is implementing financial sector initiatives including new cashless payment units and relaxed shipping documentation rules to modernize banking operations.
